free_text: Measurements of activation cross sections for the
neutron dosimetry at an energy range from 17.5 to
30 MeV by using the 7Li(p,n) quasi-mono-energetic
neutron source
Peak flux was measured with a proton recoil
telescope (PRT) technique, while the spectra were
measured with TOF technique using an NE213 detector.
Ep    Irrad.time Beam current Li thickness Peak flux
MeV   hours      mA           mm           n/cm2/sec
The Li target-sample distance is around 120 mm.
7Li(p,n) neutron spectra measured by TOF are available
in EXFOR E2489.
7Li(p,n) neutron spectra given
Decay data. Its 1998 update was also used.
Irradiated for around 12 hours
Cross sections were normalized with the peak flux
determined by the proton recoil telescope technique.
Fractional uncertainty
Corrected for low energy neutron component by using the
measured neutron spectra and evaluated or calculated
cross sections.
Uncertainty due to
- statistics of gamma-ray counting
- Ge detector efficiency calibration
- decay data (half-lives and gamma intensities)
- neutron flux determined by PRT technique
Data received from Yoshitomo Uno.
Proof-read by Yoshitomo Uno (2015-11-13)
